causes.name: Clogged Condensate Drain
causes.recommended_action: Clear the condensate drain to prevent water buildup.

causes.name: Poor Duct Insulation
causes.recommended_action: Replace or repair duct insulation to prevent condensation.

causes.name: High Humidity Levels
causes.recommended_action: Install a dehumidifier to control indoor humidity.

causes.name: Leaky Ducts
causes.recommended_action: Seal duct leaks to prevent humid air infiltration.

questions.question: Is there water pooling around the HVAC unit?
Is the duct insulation intact and dry?
Are there visible leaks in the ductwork?
Is there visible mold in or around the ducts?

diagnostic_flow.title: Inspect Condensate Drain
actions: Check for water pooling around the drain pan.
Ensure the drain line is clear of obstructions.
diagnostic_flow.field_insight: A simple visual inspection can often reveal blockages; use a wet/dry vacuum to clear minor clogs.
diagnostic_flow.interpretation: If the drain is clogged, it can cause water to back up, leading to mold growth.

diagnostic_flow.title: Evaluate Duct Insulation
actions: Inspect ductwork for missing or damaged insulation.
Measure surface temperature of ducts to check for condensation.
diagnostic_flow.field_insight: Use a thermal camera to quickly identify areas of poor insulation.
diagnostic_flow.interpretation: Poor insulation can lead to condensation, which supports mold growth.

diagnostic_flow.title: Measure Indoor Humidity
actions: Use a hygrometer to measure humidity levels in the home.
Check for signs of excess moisture in living areas.
diagnostic_flow.field_insight: Consider using a dehumidifier if humidity consistently exceeds 60%.
diagnostic_flow.interpretation: High humidity levels can exacerbate mold issues in ductwork.

diagnostic_flow.title: Inspect for Duct Leaks
actions: Perform a visual inspection for gaps or holes in ductwork.
Conduct a duct leakage test if necessary.
diagnostic_flow.field_insight: Sealing duct leaks can improve system efficiency and reduce mold risks.
diagnostic_flow.interpretation: Leaky ducts can introduce humid air, leading to condensation and mold.

narrow_down: Check if the system is receiving power
Verify airflow through vents or registers
Listen for unusual noises from the unit
Inspect for visible leaks, ice, or blockages
